operating_state: Total revenue increased 16% to $227 billion driven by 14% comparable sales growth and 26 new warehouse openings, reaching 838 locations globally with membership expanding to 65.8 million paid members. Membership fee revenue grew 9% with Executive membership penetration reaching 57% in U.S./Canada, generating 71% of worldwide net sales. Gasoline operations expanded to 14% of total net sales while e-commerce represented 7%, with ancillary businesses contributing strong services performance.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage decreased 65 basis points to 10.48% primarily from core merchandise categories pressured by fresh foods and LIFO charges reflecting inflation higher than recent years. SG&A expenses improved 77 basis points as percentage of sales to 8.88% despite multiple wage increases including minimum starting wage increases and additional paid time off benefits, with operating leverage from sales growth offsetting labor cost escalation.
material_events: Board increased quarterly dividend from $0.79 to $0.90 per share in April 2022, with total cash dividends compared to prior year including special dividend. Company paid $1.05 billion aggregate for Taiwan joint venture partner dividend and equity purchase, eliminating noncontrolling interest, while repurchasing 863,000 shares. Management repaid $800 million of Senior Notes prior to maturity in December 2021.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted net sales by $1.8 billion and gross margin, with forward contracts totaling $1.24 billion notional amount hedging international inventory purchases. Gasoline price volatility contributed 481 basis points to sales growth with 42% average price increase. Supply chain disruptions and tariff actions between China and U.S. affected merchandise costs with exposure dependent on product types and timing.
forward_outlook: Management plans approximately 29 new warehouse openings including four relocations in fiscal 2023, with capital expenditures projected at $3.8-4.0 billion financed through operating cash flows. Company maintains $2.81 billion remaining stock repurchase authorization expiring April 2023 and expects continued international expansion beyond current 260 warehouses outside the U.S. with higher growth rates in foreign markets due to smaller base operations.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple legal proceedings pending including California PAGA actions, wage and hour class actions, and opioid-related MDL litigation with unpredictable outcomes requiring potential substantial payments. Tax audits ongoing in multiple jurisdictions with gross unrecognized tax benefits recorded and exposure to additional liabilities from transfer pricing disputes. Self-insurance liabilities subject to actuarial uncertainty from inflation rates and unforeseen claim developments.
